title: Growing Overnight Culture of OP50 as Worm Food
author: Priota Islam
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bf5pjq5n

steps.title: Obtain LB Broth from the Media...
steps.instruction: Obtain LB Broth from the Media kitchen- Make sure to make at least 500ml in volume for standardisation purposes LB Broth contents: 4gNaCl 4 g Tryptone 2 g Yeast Extract dH2O to 400 mL

steps.title: Obtain sterile Erlenmeyer flask from the media kitchen
steps.instruction: Obtain sterile Erlenmeyer flask from the media kitchen

steps.title: Wipe the work area with 70%...
steps.instruction: Wipe the work area with 70% ethanol and create a relatively sterile environment on the laboratory bench by using a simple gas-powered burner

steps.title: Add the desired volume of liquid LB to the flask
steps.instruction: Add the desired volume of liquid LB to the flask

steps.title: Using a sterile inoculation loop, select...
steps.instruction: Using a sterile inoculation loop, select a single colony from your bacteria streaked LB agar plate (One streaked plate can be used for about a month and stored at 4C during that time period)

steps.title: Dip the inoculation loop into the liquid LB and swirl
steps.instruction: Dip the inoculation loop into the liquid LB and swirl. Discard the inoculation loop

steps.title: Loosely cover the culture with sterile...
steps.instruction: Loosely cover the culture with sterile aluminium foil or a cap that is not air tight as bacteria needs air

steps.title: Incubate the bacterial culture at the...
steps.instruction: Incubate the bacterial culture at the required growth temperature overnight in a shaking incubator

steps.title: After incubation, check for growth, which...
steps.instruction: After incubation, check for growth, which is characterized by a cloudy haze in the media

steps.title: Measure the optical density of the...
steps.instruction: Measure the optical density of the bacterial culture at 600nm wavelength using a spectrophotometer. Record the OD600 three times and calculate average, use LB Broth as Blank
